title: Christmas Show

jokes.text: Deck the slopes with babes in tight pants / Carlton's gonna get some romance / No, you're not, 'cause you're too ugly / They'll all be in my room, cute and snugly
jokes.setup: Carlton singing confidently about getting romance on the ski trip
jokes.punchline: Will's brutal shutdown calling Carlton too ugly
jokes.explanation: Strong character dynamic - Will's street-smart cynicism vs Carlton's naive optimism. The parody song format elevates it above a simple insult.

jokes.text: WiII, is that aII you're taking for the entire Christmas vacation? No, man. These just my hair products.
jokes.setup: Phil asks if Will's small bag is all he's bringing
jokes.punchline: Reveals it's just hair products, implying more luggage to come
jokes.explanation: Classic Will character beat - his vanity and style consciousness. Economical setup/payoff.

jokes.text: What about your ski cIothes? CarIton... skiing is for white guys named Sven... and O.J. Simpson.
jokes.setup: Carlton asks about ski clothes
jokes.punchline: Will's racial observation with unexpected O.J. addition
jokes.explanation: Strong cultural commentary with unexpected punchline. O.J. reference adds specificity and surprise.

jokes.text: This is L.A., man, if I want to get my head cracked... I can star in the next Rodney King video.
jokes.setup: Carlton describes skiing dangers of slamming into trees
jokes.punchline: Will's dark comparison to police brutality
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy that tackles serious social issues with perfect timing. The juxtaposition of vacation activities with police brutality is both shocking and insightful.

jokes.text: Forget it, AshIey, I am not getting you a stun gun for Christmas. I'm not reaIIy going to use it on Cousin Bobby... I just want to scare him with it.
jokes.setup: Phil refuses to buy Ashley a stun gun
jokes.punchline: Ashley's casual admission she wants to threaten family
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Ashley's mischievous side. The casual violence threat from a young girl creates the humor.

jokes.text: Yeah, right, and maybe it was Barbie's idea to catch that tan in the microwave.
jokes.setup: Phil suggests maybe they remember Cousin Bobby worse than he is
jokes.punchline: Ashley's sarcastic comparison to a melted Barbie doll
jokes.explanation: Clever visual metaphor that's both specific and absurd. The image of a microwaved Barbie is memorable and perfectly conveys Ashley's skepticism.

jokes.text: Daddy, these tickets are aII wrong. They Iook aII right to me, sweetheart. But Daddy, they're... coach. Better watch it, HiI. Next thing you know, you'II be riding the... bus.
jokes.setup: Hilary complains about coach tickets being wrong
jokes.punchline: Phil's threat of even lower-class transportation
jokes.explanation: Classic Hilary character beat - her horror at anything less than first-class. Phil's escalation threat works well with her privileged attitude.

jokes.text: Better watch it, Hil. Next thing you know, you'll be riding the... bus.
jokes.setup: Hilary's horror at flying coach
jokes.punchline: Will threatening even worse transportation - the bus
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Hilary's privilege panic. The pause before 'bus' sells the horror of the concept.

jokes.text: Baby, we're on vacation. Wherever we Iie down, we aren't gonna be sIeeping. Lester... how many times I gotta teII you to cut down on that vitamin E? I am not a machine.
jokes.setup: Lester makes sexual innuendo about not sleeping on vacation
jokes.punchline: Helen's complaint about his excessive libido and vitamin E
jokes.explanation: Adult humor that plays on the vitamin E stereotype. Helen's exasperated response sells the running gag of Lester's high libido.

jokes.text: WeII, I am. You gonna Iet me rock your worId? Get a grip on yourseIf, Lester. Don't encourage him.
jokes.setup: Helen says she's not a machine
jokes.punchline: Lester doubles down with 'rock your world' followed by Helen's shutdown
jokes.explanation: Continuation of the Lester/Helen dynamic. The escalation works but feels somewhat predictable.

jokes.text: No, no, baby, it Iooks great. Everything is great. Even your Aunt HeIen's mustache is great.
jokes.setup: Vy reassures Will his mustache looks great
jokes.punchline: Her over-the-top positivity extends to insulting Aunt Helen
jokes.explanation: Good escalation joke that reveals Vy's unnaturally positive state while getting in a dig at Helen. The structure builds nicely.

jokes.text: Is it haIf fuII of Scotch?
jokes.setup: Vy uses the glass half-full optimism cliché
jokes.punchline: Will asks if it's half-full of alcohol, explaining her attitude
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and timing. Will's practical explanation for his mother's unusual optimism lands well.

jokes.text: I thought you said, 'Next time I see that white boy, I hope he's under a bus.' That was just a figure of speech.
jokes.setup: Frank questions Vy's warm greeting
jokes.punchline: Reveals her previous death wish was 'just a figure of speech'
jokes.explanation: Great character consistency callback that shows family dysfunction while highlighting Vy's current unnaturally positive state.

jokes.text: But for $10, you can use my sIeeping bag.
jokes.setup: Bobby reveals he got the last bed, leaving Phil without one
jokes.punchline: Casually offers to rent Phil his sleeping bag
jokes.explanation: Good character establishment for Bobby as opportunistic and mercenary, even with family.

jokes.text: I never guaranteed anyone anything. That's my motto: 'Never guarantee anyone anything... and beware of cross-eyed women.'
jokes.setup: Agent denies guaranteeing anything about the rental
jokes.punchline: His bizarre life motto about cross-eyed women
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully random character detail that establishes the agent as eccentric. The specificity of 'cross-eyed women' makes it memorable.

jokes.text: WeII, I guess those cross-eyed women are doing something right.
jokes.setup: Agent says there's not an empty bed in the whole state
jokes.punchline: Implies cross-eyed women are responsible for the bed shortage
jokes.explanation: Nice callback that connects his random motto to the current situation. The innuendo lands subtly.

jokes.text: What do you mean, friend? You mean like, friend... or friend?
jokes.setup: Vy mentioning Robert is a 'friend'
jokes.punchline: Will's distinction between platonic and romantic 'friend'
jokes.explanation: Classic protective son moment. The emphasis on 'friend' conveys the innuendo well.

jokes.text: Has Robert seen your moves?
jokes.setup: Vy talks about showing Will her ski moves
jokes.punchline: Will's double entendre about her mysterious boyfriend
jokes.explanation: Will's jealousy about his mother's boyfriend expressed through innuendo. Character-appropriate but predictable.

jokes.text: Daddy. When you were roIIing down that big hiII... you Iooked just Iike Gumby. Yeah, UncIe Les. And you sounded Iike Patti LaBeIIe.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil has clearly had a skiing accident
jokes.punchline: Ashley and Will describe his appearance and sounds during the fall
jokes.explanation: Great visual comedy description. Gumby comparison for his floppiness and Patti LaBelle for his screaming creates vivid mental image.

jokes.text: Yeah, Uncle Les. And you sounded like Patti LaBelle.
jokes.setup: Ashley's Gumby comment about Phil's crash
jokes.punchline: Will adding that he sounded like Patti LaBelle
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that adds audio to Ashley's visual. Patti LaBelle reference suggests high-pitched screaming.

jokes.text: This Robert must have a magic wand.
jokes.setup: Vy talks about love conquering all with unusual positivity
jokes.punchline: Will's sexual innuendo about Robert's effect on his mother
jokes.explanation: Will's continued discomfort with his mother's relationship expressed through innuendo. Well-timed but not especially original.

jokes.text: Oh, grow up.
jokes.setup: Will explains pregnancy test procedure in euphemistic terms
jokes.punchline: Ashley's mature dismissal of his childishness
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Ashley as more mature than Will. Economical but not particularly memorable.

jokes.text: The onIy bIack peopIe I've seen been in the Ray CharIes Pepsi commerciaI. You're kidding. Every time I turn around, I see a bIack person.
jokes.setup: Will complains about lack of Black people in Utah
jokes.punchline: Carlton's oblivious response while surrounded by his own Black family
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton moment showing his disconnect from Black identity and culture. The irony is well-constructed and character-revealing.

jokes.text: You're kidding. Every time I turn around, I see a black person.
jokes.setup: Will's comment about lack of black people in Utah
jokes.punchline: Carlton's response showing he only sees his own family
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton response showing his sheltered perspective. The literal interpretation of Will's observation is very in-character.

jokes.text: Come on, girI. This is a pregnancy test, not an order of nachos.
jokes.setup: Janice wants everyone to share the pregnancy test moment with her
jokes.punchline: Will's comparison to shareable food
jokes.explanation: Excellent analogy that highlights the absurdity of group pregnancy test viewing. The specificity of nachos makes it memorable.

jokes.text: Hey, come on, one-Ieg daddy!
jokes.setup: Phil and Vivian are dancing to oldies music
jokes.punchline: Helen references Phil's skiing injury while encouraging him to dance
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Phil's injury while maintaining the party atmosphere. Helen's encouraging tone makes it playful rather than mean.

jokes.text: It's oId time at the ApoIIo!
jokes.setup: The family is dancing and performing
jokes.punchline: Will compares it to the famous Harlem theater's amateur night
jokes.explanation: Solid cultural reference that fits Will's character and the situation. Works as both celebration and gentle mockery.

jokes.text: That's probabIy Donny and Marie coming over to borrow a cup of rhythm.
jokes.setup: Someone knocks at the door unexpectedly
jokes.punchline: Will's joke about the white Utah entertainment duo needing rhythm
jokes.explanation: Brilliant cultural reference that plays on multiple levels - Utah setting, racial dynamics, and the 'borrowing a cup of sugar' cliché. Very quotable.

jokes.text: CarIton, you suburban twit, this guy's a stranger.
jokes.setup: Carlton wants to let the stranger in from the cold
jokes.punchline: Will's characteristic insult highlighting Carlton's naivety
jokes.explanation: Classic Will/Carlton dynamic. 'Suburban twit' is a perfect character-specific insult that captures their class and street-smart differences.

jokes.text: UnIess he is on a muIe with a pregnant virgin... he ain't getting in here.
jokes.setup: Family argues about letting stranger in for Christmas
jokes.punchline: Will's nativity scene reference setting impossible standards
jokes.explanation: Exceptional joke that cleverly references the Christmas story while establishing Will's street-smart suspicion. Perfect economy and memorable phrasing.

jokes.text: Hey, my man, ArnoId. Why don't you say Merry Christmas for the camera? Merry Christmas. CouId I say one more thing? Yeah. Hurry up though, I'm about to run out of tape. Okay. Nobody move, this is a stick-up.
jokes.setup: Will films Arnold saying Merry Christmas, very friendly and innocent
jokes.punchline: Arnold reveals he's robbing them, subverting the wholesome moment
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection that subverts audience expectations. The friendly Christmas greeting makes the robbery reveal much more shocking and effective.

jokes.text: The creep even took the toiIet paper. Don't ask me how I know.
jokes.setup: Family discovers extent of what was stolen
jokes.punchline: Will's TMI about discovering the missing toilet paper
jokes.explanation: Good bathroom humor that implies without being explicit. The 'don't ask' construction works well.

jokes.text: The creep even took the toilet paper. / Don't ask me how I know.
jokes.setup: Cataloging what the robber stole
jokes.punchline: Will's implication about bathroom discovery
jokes.explanation: Good gross-out humor that implies Will's bathroom experience. The 'don't ask' makes it funnier by not explaining.

jokes.text: WeII, maybe he'II caII in with the resuIts.
jokes.setup: Janice mentions the thief took her pregnancy test
jokes.punchline: Will suggests the thief might report the results
jokes.explanation: Absurd but logical extension of the situation. The idea of a thief calling with medical results is wonderfully ridiculous.

jokes.text: Wait a minute, I thought you were the reaI estate agent. I am. I'm aIso the Sheriff... notary pubIic... mortician... and I'm a pretty good IittIe dancer, too.
jokes.setup: Will questions the agent's dual role as sheriff
jokes.punchline: Agent lists increasingly random jobs, ending with dancing
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that establishes the small town setting through absurd multi-tasking. The dancing addition provides perfect cap.

jokes.text: Looks like you folks have been picked cleaner than Pavarotti's chicken bones.
jokes.setup: Sheriff observing the robbery scene
jokes.punchline: Comparison to opera singer's eating habits
jokes.explanation: Creative metaphor connecting classical music with thorough eating. Shows the sheriff's colorful speech patterns.

jokes.text: Let me guess... there's a Iot of marriage between first cousins in this town, right?
jokes.setup: Sheriff reveals he has multiple seemingly incompatible jobs
jokes.punchline: Will's inbred small-town stereotype observation
jokes.explanation: Will's street-smart cultural observation about small town stereotypes. Character-appropriate but fairly predictable.

jokes.text: Let me guess... there's a lot of marriage between first cousins in this town, right?
jokes.setup: Sheriff revealing he has multiple jobs in town
jokes.punchline: Will's inbred population joke
jokes.explanation: Standard small town joke but delivered with Will's characteristic bluntness. Not particularly original but fits the character.

jokes.text: What the heII is a boom box?
jokes.setup: Will mentions the thief took his boom box
jokes.punchline: Sheriff's complete ignorance of basic 90s technology
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing the cultural gap between Will and small-town Utah. Simple but effective.

jokes.text: Buy all new stuff.
jokes.setup: Family asking about chances of getting their stuff back
jokes.punchline: Sheriff's blunt assessment of their prospects
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery of bad news. The economy of words makes it funnier than a long explanation.

jokes.text: WeII, I'm not sIeeping with anyone but Lester. Except maybe DenzeI Washington. Yes, ma'am !
jokes.setup: Helen declares loyalty to sleeping only with Lester
jokes.punchline: Makes exception for Denzel, Lester enthusiastically agrees
jokes.explanation: Nice couple dynamic where Lester's eagerness to agree makes it funnier. Denzel is a good celebrity choice.

jokes.text: No, CarIton, I'm doing homeboy yoga.
jokes.setup: Carlton asks if Will is asleep while he's lying on the floor
jokes.punchline: Will's sarcastic response about 'homeboy yoga'
jokes.explanation: Excellent Will line that combines his sarcasm with a clever cultural mashup. 'Homeboy yoga' is a perfect Will Smith coinage.

jokes.text: Yeah, CarIton, you're scared of zipper-fIy jeans.
jokes.setup: Carlton admits to being scared during the robbery
jokes.punchline: Will mocks Carlton's fears with absurd example
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton-specific insult that captures his sheltered, fearful nature. The specificity of zipper-fly jeans makes it memorable.

jokes.text: If AshIey hadn't sIapped me, man, I might have screamed.
jokes.setup: Will admits he was also scared during the robbery
jokes.punchline: Reveals Ashley had to snap him out of panic
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Will's vulnerability while crediting Ashley's quick thinking. Humanizes Will after his bravado.

jokes.text: Aren't you gonna say it back?
jokes.setup: Carlton declares love, Will doesn't respond
jokes.punchline: Carlton's persistent need for reciprocation increases the awkwardness
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that builds the cringe comedy. Carlton's neediness is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: If there is an ounce of decency Ieft in you... just teII him you Iove him.
jokes.setup: Extended awkward silence between Will and Carlton
jokes.punchline: Ashley's exasperated intervention to end the painful moment
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff to the extended cringe sequence. Ashley becomes the audience surrogate, unable to stand the awkwardness.

jokes.text: I Iove you, man. AII right? I Iove you! I Iove you!
jokes.setup: Ashley pressures Will to reciprocate Carlton's feelings
jokes.punchline: Will's explosive, over-the-top declaration to end the discomfort
jokes.explanation: Great payoff that shows Will's emotional walls breaking down. The repetition and escalation work perfectly for the character moment.

jokes.text: This is the worst damn Christmas I've ever had! And Iook at this fooI.
jokes.setup: Phil drops his positive front about their terrible Christmas
jokes.punchline: Points to someone (likely Carlton) doing something foolish
jokes.explanation: Phil's breaking point moment. Works as character beat but the visual reference is unclear from transcript alone.

jokes.text: Hot water and chewing gum, sir.
jokes.setup: Phil asks Geoffrey what's for breakfast after being robbed
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's deadpan description of their meager remaining supplies
jokes.explanation: Perfect Geoffrey deadpan delivery. The absurd breakfast menu delivered with butler formality is excellent character work.

jokes.text: Yeah, she couIdn't afford their cIothes, so she toId us they were topIess.
jokes.setup: Vy remembers Christmas when her mother got laid off and brought home dolls
jokes.punchline: Mother's creative explanation for why the dolls were naked
jokes.explanation: Excellent story that shows both poverty and parental creativity. The 'topless' reframing is both funny and touching.

jokes.text: Does that mean you're gonna go out and make me a Miata?
jokes.setup: Phil shares story about parents making drums from pots and pans
jokes.punchline: Ashley asks if he'll similarly construct her a luxury car
jokes.explanation: Smart joke that shows Ashley's materialism while cleverly extending Phil's nostalgic story. The specificity of Miata makes it funnier.

jokes.text: For my Christmas gift... I'm gonna give AshIey a noogie.
jokes.setup: Family is sharing meaningful Christmas gifts of personal growth
jokes.punchline: Will's immature physical gesture breaks the sentimental mood
jokes.explanation: Perfect Will moment that undercuts the saccharine sentiment with typical big brother behavior. Character-appropriate mood breaker.

jokes.text: I am going to give Bobby five seconds... before I ram that video game down his throat!
jokes.setup: Ashley starts to give her meaningful Christmas gift
jokes.punchline: Threat of violence against Bobby interrupts her sentiment
jokes.explanation: Good subversion of the sentimental moment showing Ashley's violent streak. The threat specificity makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Now, there's a gift for the person who has everything.
jokes.setup: Janice announces she's going to throw up
jokes.punchline: Frank's dry comment about vomit as a gift
jokes.explanation: Nice dry humor that reframes morning sickness as a gift. Frank's deadpan delivery style works well here.

jokes.text: Thanks, baby, but I aIready have one of you.
jokes.setup: Will offers himself as a Christmas gift to his mother
jokes.punchline: Vy's gentle rejection implying one Will is enough
jokes.explanation: Perfect mother-son dynamic. Vy's loving but realistic response to Will's gesture is both sweet and funny.

jokes.text: You ain't no good at picking Christmas gifts, neither.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey offers hand towels as his Christmas gift and admits he's no good at mushy stuff
jokes.punchline: Will's critique of Geoffrey's gift-giving skills
jokes.explanation: Good callback that maintains Will's sarcastic commentary on Geoffrey's practical but unromantic gift choice.

jokes.text: Baby Jesus went through the windshieId and knocked him out coId.
jokes.setup: Sheriff explains the thief crashed into courthouse nativity scene
jokes.punchline: Baby Jesus statue becomes an unlikely weapon of justice
jokes.explanation: Absolutely brilliant absurdist humor that turns religious imagery into slapstick justice. The phrase 'Baby Jesus went through the windshield' is comedy gold.

jokes.text: No, no, CarIton, you can stiII sing soIo... so Iow that we can't hear you.
jokes.setup: Carlton complains his Christmas gift solo is being drowned out
jokes.punchline: Will's pun on 'solo' and 'so low' to insult Carlton's singing
jokes.explanation: Classic Will pun that's both clever wordplay and character-appropriate putdown of Carlton. The economy is perfect.

tmdb_overview: On a family ski trip, the Banks find that they must come up with Christmas gifts from the heart after their belongings are stolen.
